title: Board Policies
description: Board Policy BF covers the general framework for how the school board adopts, organizes, and maintains its policies. It addresses the relationship between different types of board policies and the process for making amendments. The policy also outlines how updates to the policy system are incorporated after adoption.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Board Policy BF (Local) streamlines language around legally referenced policies by removing references to them being "not adopted by the Board" and eliminating the instruction to read legal and local policies together, while also removing the requirement that policy amendments be generally recommended by the Superintendent. The section heading "TASB Localized Policy Updates" is updated and the phrase "of legally referenced policies" is removed from the post-adoption incorporation language.
timeline_local.changes_added: ["d policies" (replacing "d policies" — the heading "Organization" is struck and the text "Legally referencesd policies" reflects a redline correction to "Legally referenced policies")]
changes_removed: Legally referencesd policies are not adopted by the Board.
At each policy code the legally referenced policy and the Board-adopted local policy must be read together to further a full understanding of a topic.
but generally shall be recommended for the Board's consideration by the Superintendent.
TASB Localized Policy Updates (section heading — struck in red)
timeline_local.notes: The redline scan quality is somewhat low and the red text is partially difficult to distinguish from black in certain areas. The section headings "Organization" and "TASB Localized Policy Updates" appear in red, suggesting they are being changed or removed/replaced, but no clear red-underlined replacement text is visible for those headings. The "changes_added" array is minimal because very little new underlined text was identifiable — primarily the typographic correction to "Legally referencesd" appears as a tracked change artifact rather than a substantive addition. The main substantive changes are all removals (strikethroughs).
